question: What are identification numbers of my jetski? I don't understand that.
content:
The identification numbers are the three numbers Yamaha wants you to record for parts orders and theft records: the Primary Identification (PRI-ID) number, the Hull Identification Number (HIN), and the engine serial number.
1. The PRI-ID number is stamped on a label inside the engine compartment; the figure marks that label as item 1.<PIC>
2. The HIN is stamped on a plate attached to the hull on the aft, starboard (right) side; the figure marks that plate as item 1.<PIC>
3. The engine serial number is stamped on a label attached to the engine unit; the figure marks that label as item 1.<PIC>
Write all three down and keep a separate copy somewhere safe in case the watercraft is stolen.
ret:

1. The PRI-ID number is stamped on a label inside the engine compartment; the figure marks that label as item 1.<PIC>
2. The HIN is stamped on a plate attached to the hull on the aft, starboard (right) side; the figure marks that plate as item 1.<PIC>
3. The engine serial number is stamped on a label attached to the engine unit; the figure marks that label as item 1.<PIC>
Write all three down and keep a separate copy somewhere safe in case the watercraft is stolen.",["jetski_01", "jetski_02", "jetski_03"]
notes: Answered from the WaveRunner identification-number section. Images use the location diagrams adjacent to the PRI-ID, HIN, and engine serial number descriptions.
